quiz.title: Unit 1: Introduction to Life Science - Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Unit Review
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Characteristics of Life, Lab Skills & Scientific Methods

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic shared by all living organisms?
questions.explanation: While many living things move, it is not a defining characteristic. Plants, for example, grow and respond to stimuli but do not move from place to place. Reproduction, homeostasis, and cellular organization are universal characteristics of life.

questions.question: What is the first step in the scientific method?
questions.explanation: The scientific method begins with an observation that leads to a question. A hypothesis is then proposed as a testable explanation for that observation.

questions.question: In an experiment testing the effect of fertilizer on plant growth, what is the independent variable?
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor that the scientist deliberately changes. In this case, the type of fertilizer is being manipulated to see its effect on plant growth.

questions.question: What should you do FIRST if a chemical spills on your skin in the lab?
options: Wipe it off with a paper towel
Flush the area with water for 15 minutes
Continue working and clean up later
questions.explanation: Immediate and prolonged flushing with water is the critical first aid for a chemical spill on skin. You should notify your teacher after or while beginning to flush the area.

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the seven characteristics of life?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a process carried out by some organisms (plants and algae) to obtain energy, but it is not a universal characteristic of life. All living things must perform cellular organization, metabolism, and reproduction, but not all perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which part of the microscope controls the amount of light reaching the specimen?
questions.explanation: The diaphragm (or iris diaphragm) is located under the stage and can be opened or closed to adjust the intensity and angle of light reaching the specimen.

questions.question: When preparing a wet mount slide, why should you lower the coverslip at an angle?
options: To make the slide look neater
To prevent air bubbles from forming
questions.explanation: Lowering the coverslip gently at a 45-degree angle allows the water to spread out and push air to the side, minimizing the formation of air bubbles that can obscure the view.

questions.question: Which unit is MOST appropriate for measuring the mass of a small insect?
questions.explanation: A small insect, like an ant or a fly, typically has a mass measured in milligrams (mg). Grams would be suitable for larger insects, and kilograms are for very large objects. Liters measure volume, not mass.

questions.question: What type of graph is BEST for showing the relationship between two continuous variables?
questions.explanation: A scatter plot is specifically designed to display the relationship between two continuous variables (e.g., height vs. weight, time vs. temperature) and can reveal trends or correlations.

questions.question: Which characteristic of life allows organisms to maintain a stable internal environment despite external changes?
questions.explanation: Homeostasis is the process by which living organisms maintain a stable internal environment (e.g., constant body temperature, pH, water balance) even when external conditions change. This is essential for proper functioning and survival.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the core concepts of Unit 1.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review the details on experimental variables and lab safety.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the characteristics of life and microscope basics.
scoreMessages.0: Please review all sections of Unit 1: Introduction to Life Science.
